slos.sli_definition: 99th percentile request latency at or below target
slos.rationale: The observed p99 latency is 50.0ms with a stddev of 10.5ms across 60000000 requests. The tight stddev (21% of p99) and high sample count indicate stable, predictable latency behavior. Setting the target at 60.5ms provides 1 stddev headroom. The p50-to-p99 ratio and high throughput suggest this gateway is well-optimized. Consider tightening to 55ms once stability is confirmed over 90 days.

slos.sli_definition: Ratio of successful requests at or above target
slos.rationale: The observed availability is 0.9999 (99.99%) with error rate stddev of 0.00004. Out of 60000000 requests, only 6000 failed. Setting the target at 0.99982 provides 2 stddev headroom. The extremely low error rate variance suggests highly reliable error handling. This target is achievable and accounts for rare transient failures during deployments.
